explanations.27: useBlogPost() is a hook that gives us access to the current blog post's data—think of it as reaching into Docusaurus to grab the post's metadata.
explanations.32: This checks if there's an updates array in the frontMatter (the YAML metadata at the top of a markdown file) and if it has at least one item.
explanations.34: We're spreading the updates array to make a copy, then sorting it so newest dates come first, then grabbing the first one—this finds the most recent update date.
explanations.50: clsx() is a utility that lets us combine multiple CSS class names together—in this case, Docusaurus's alert styles plus our custom styles.
explanations.54: aria-hidden='true' tells screen readers to ignore the emoji so they don't hear 'warning sign' twice (since the text already says the message).
